--- title: 5.2. PLSR vs. CNN Validation Curves keywords: fastai sidebar: home_sidebar summary: "Visualizing the behaviours (performances) of PLSR and CNN models as their capacity increases" description: "Visualizing the behaviours (performances) of PLSR and CNN models as their capacity increases" nb_path: "nbs/20_paper.figures.validation_curves.ipynb" ---
{% raw %}
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2

The autoreload extension is already loaded. To reload it, use:
  %reload_ext autoreload
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
from pathlib import Path
import pickle

from mirzai.vis.core import (centimeter, PRIMARY_COLOR, 
                             plot_validation_curve,
                             set_style, DEFAULT_STYLE)

# Data vis.
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from matplotlib.gridspec import GridSpec

# Data science stack
import numpy as np

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
{% endraw %}

Input data

To generate the learning curves for both the PLSR and CNN models, run the following notebooks:

  • PLSR Validation curve
  • CNN Validation curve

Instead, we load already generated and saved data: history_pls_validation_curve.pickle and history_cnn_validation_curve.pickle.

{% raw %} 
src_dir = Path('./files/dumps')
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
fnames = ['history_pls_validation_curve.pickle', 'history_cnn_validation_curve.pickle'] 
history_pls, history_cnn = [pickle.load(open(src_dir/fname, "rb")) for fname in fnames]
{% endraw %}

Plot

{% raw %} 
def plot_validation_curves(history_pls, history_cnn,
                           figsize=(16*centimeter,6*centimeter), dpi=600):
    # Layout 
    fig = plt.figure(figsize=figsize, dpi=600)

    gs = GridSpec(nrows=1, ncols=2)

    ax0 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 0])
    ax0.set_title('(a)', loc='left')
    ax0.set_ylim([0.025, 0.09]) 

    ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0, 1], sharey=ax0)
    ax1.set_title('(b)', loc='left')

    # Plots
    for (ax, history) in zip([ax0, ax1], [history_pls, history_cnn]):
        x, losses_train, losses_valid = history.values()
        plot_params = {'color': 'C0', 'zorder': 2, 'label': 'Training'} 
        fill_between_params = {'facecolor': 'C0', 'alpha': 0.25, 'zorder': 1}
        plot_validation_curve(x, losses_train, ax=ax,
                              plot_kwargs=plot_params, 
                              fill_between_kwargs=fill_between_params)

        plot_params = {'color': 'C1', 'zorder': 4, 'label': 'Validation'} 
        fill_between_params = {'facecolor': 'C1', 'alpha': 0.25, 'zorder': 3}
        plot_validation_curve(x, losses_valid, ax=ax,
                              plot_kwargs=plot_params, 
                              fill_between_kwargs=fill_between_params)

    # # Ornaments
    ax0.legend(loc='best', frameon=False) 
    ax0.set_ylabel('Mean Squared Error →', loc='top')
    ax0.set_xlabel('Number of PLS components →', loc='right')
    ax1.set_xlabel('Number of epochs →', loc='right')
    for ax in [ax0, ax1]:
        ax.grid(True, "minor", color="0.85", linewidth=0.2, zorder=-2)
        ax.grid(True, "major", color="0.65", linewidth=0.4, zorder=-1) 

    plt.tight_layout()
{% endraw %} {% raw %} 
FIG_PATH = Path('nameofyourfolder')
set_style(DEFAULT_STYLE)
plot_validation_curves(history_pls, history_cnn)

# To save/export it
# plt.savefig(FIG_PATH/'validation-curves.png', dpi=600, transparent=True, format='png')
{% endraw %}
